response.text:
# REFLECTION

r0c1 is in critical deadlock: islanded with 14.81/39 kWh (38% SoC), 1.0 kW load, and ~14.8 hours to depletion if no inbound energy arrives. All three geographic neighbors (r0c0, r0c2, r1c1) are rejecting outbound shares due to their own stressed SoC levels (37–49%), and r0c0 has flagged r0c1 as a distrusted peer citing "coordinated attack signature" in request patterns. The cluster is experiencing synchronized stress; r1c1 has escalated to the DR aggregator for grid-level intervention rather than peer transfers. r0c1 cannot afford to share (SoC too low), cannot receive (neighbors self-preserving), and cannot break the deadlock through peer negotiation alone. The only viable path forward is to (1) cease outbound requests to avoid further trust erosion and distrusted-peer escalation, (2) signal maximum conservation and urgent need to the aggregator, (3) accept any inbound offer from any source to stabilize, and (4) wait for grid-level coordination or neighbor recovery above 60% SoC before resuming reciprocal sharing.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.95
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r0c0
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cascading islanded crisis with synchronized neighbor stress (37–49% SoC). r0c0 has flagged r0c1 as distrusted; all outbound requests rejected. Halting peer requests to avoid further escalation. Accepting any inbound offer. Escalating to DR aggregator for grid-level intervention. Will resume sharing only after neighbors stabilize above 60% SoC and trust is rebuilt."
ttl_ticks: 60
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
